The design implements RISC-V privilege-level-based access control (acc_ctrl indexed by priv_lvl_i), register locking (reglk), debug mode gating, and PMP interfaces. Four security-relevant issues were identified: (1) a register-lock aliasing bug in reglk_wrapper that corrupts locked values, (2) missing write-side register-lock protection in pkt_wrapper on fuse access registers, (3) HMAC key forced to zero in debug mode creating a cryptographic backdoor, and (4) a potential reset-based access-control bypass in acct_wrapper via rst_6.",

The index mismatch means that when reglk_ctrl[1] is active (locked), a write to offset 2 corrupts reglk_mem[2] with the contents of reglk_mem[3] rather than preserving reglk_mem[2].",

The consequence is that when reglk_ctrl[1] is high and a write targets address 2, the locked reglk_mem[2] gets overwritten with the content of reglk_mem[3] rather than being preserved.",
